Title: Engineering a Plant Polyketide Synthase for the Biosynthesis of Methylated Flavonoids
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase (chalcone synthase family); typical αβαβα pseudosymmetric thiolase motif in C-terminal domain and α-helical motif in N-terminal domain
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible T7-based plasmid expression from pETDuet-1/pET28a constructs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: chalcone/flavanone formation from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA starter units; methylated flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate chalcone precursor of the target flavanones and determines acceptance of O-methylated starter substrates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA (including feruloyl-CoA and isoferuloyl-CoA-derived starter units) with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone intermediates that are subsequently isomerized to flavanones such as homoeriodictyol and hesperetin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: chalcone intermediates leading to homoeriodictyol and hesperetin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: engineered E. coli culture broth (extracellular fermentation products measured)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: health-promoting O-methylated flavonoids with bioactivity relevant to treating human diseases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in engineered E. coli after IPTG induction during fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key control/first committed-step enzyme for methylated flavonoid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type HvCHS supports methylated flavonoid production in E. coli, and engineered HvCHS variants increase homoeriodictyol and hesperetin titers; double mutant Q232P,D234V gives the best performance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway reconstruction in E. coli; site-directed mutagenesis; small-scale and shake-flask fermentations; HPLC/HPLC-MS metabolite profiling; X-ray crystal structures; in vitro steady-state enzyme kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fed ferulic acid or isoferulic acid precursors (1 mM); IPTG induction; cerulenin supplementation; fermentation for 32 h
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: strong preference for ferulic-acid-derived homoeriodictyol over isoferulic-acid-derived hesperetin limits hesperetin yield; no obvious growth penalty in E. coli based on similar OD600 among strains
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime enzyme engineering target for microbial production of methylated flavonoids; loop engineering and especially Q232P+D234V improve titers; useful for rational engineering/directed evolution and pathway optimization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structures deposited as PDB 8B32, 8B35, 8B3C. O-methylated flavonoids impaired soaking-based crystallography. Surface loop residues distant from active site unexpectedly affected in vivo performance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvCHS was identified as the first committed-step enzyme controlling methylated flavonoid biosynthesis in engineered E. coli. 2. Structural analysis plus mutagenesis revealed that active-site residue A199 and surface loop residues influence acceptance of O-methylated substrates. 3. HvCHS(Q232P,D234V) produced the highest homoeriodictyol and hesperetin titers and showed increased affinity toward feruloyl-CoA.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase (chalcone synthase family)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible T7-based plasmid expression from pETDuet-1/pET28a constructs
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: first committed step; engineered control point
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly enhances flux to methylated flavanones by improving enzyme performance toward methylated substrates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes chalcone formation from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA starter units and malonyl-CoA, with improved performance toward methylated substrate-derived product formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: chalcone intermediates leading to homoeriodictyol and hesperetin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: health-promoting O-methylated flavonoids with disease-related bioactivities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in engineered E. coli after IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: engineered bottleneck-improving variant of the first committed-step enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Best-performing variant; increased final homoeriodictyol titers about 2-fold and hesperetin titers about 3-fold versus wild-type HvCHS in small-scale tests; in larger-scale fermentation achieved 0.33 mM homoeriodictyol and 0.016 mM hesperetin, exceeding previous report by about 2-fold and 10-fold, respectively
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; heterologous expression in E. coli; shake-flask and small-scale fermentation; HPLC quantification; purified enzyme kinetics comparing wild type and mutant
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fed ferulic acid or isoferulic acid; IPTG induction; cerulenin supplementation; 32 h fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: hesperetin production still remained much lower than homoeriodictyol because of persistent substrate preference; no growth defect reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineered enzyme for microbial cell factories producing O-methylated flavonoids; suitable template for further directed evolution/rational engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Enhanced biosynthesis likely reflects both higher protein abundance and a 1.6-fold lower apparent KM for feruloyl-CoA, while vmax and kcat remained similar to wild type
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Combining Q232P and D234V in a surface loop gave the highest methylated flavonoid titers among tested CHS variants. 2. The double mutant improved affinity for feruloyl-CoA and likely increased protein abundance/solubility. 3. This variant enabled the best reported pathway performance in this study for homoeriodictyol and hesperetin.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase (chalcone synthase family)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible T7-based plasmid expression from pETDuet-1 constructs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: enables acceptance of O-methylated precursor for downstream hesperetin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes chalcone formation from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA starter units and malonyl-CoA; T197A broadens substrate acceptance toward O-methylated precursor-derived hesperetin formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: chalcone intermediates leading to hesperetin and homoeriodictyol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: health-promoting O-methylated flavonoids with disease-related bioactivities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in engineered E. coli after IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key substrate-selectivity determinant within committed-step enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: T197A enabled hesperetin production for the first time with PhCHS, while homoeriodictyol production remained comparable to wild-type PhCHS strain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; heterologous pathway reconstruction in E. coli; fermentation with fed precursors; HPLC metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fed ferulic acid or isoferulic acid; IPTG induction; cerulenin supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: absolute production remained below best HvCHS-based engineered strain
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates single-residue engineering can unlock methylated substrate utilization in canonical CHS enzymes; useful starting point for further engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states this mutation enabled hesperetin production with this enzyme for the first time
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A single active-site substitution, T197A, in PhCHS enabled production of hesperetin from O-methylated precursor. 2. Homoeriodictyol titer remained similar to wild-type PhCHS. 3. The result supports alanine at this position as a determinant for isoferulic-acid-derived product formation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible T7-based plasmid expression from pETDuet-1/pCDFDuet-1/pET28a constructs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: hydroxycinnamic acid activation supplying methylated flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies activated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA starter units required for downstream CHS-catalyzed methylated flavonoid formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids such as ferulic acid and isoferulic acid to the corresponding CoA thioesters using ATP and CoA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ferulic acid; isoferulic acid; ATP; coenzyme A
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: health-promoting O-methylated flavonoids with disease-related bioactivities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in engineered E. coli after IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established as principal bottleneck in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Os4CL with HvCHS supported higher homoeriodictyol production than PhCHS/Pc4CL combination and was the only combination producing hesperetin in the initial comparison
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway reconstruction in E. coli; mutagenesis screening of 4CL variants; in vitro synthesis of feruloyl-CoA; HPLC metabolite analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fed ferulic acid or isoferulic acid; IPTG induction; cerulenin supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 4CL mutagenesis gave only modest benefits compared with CHS engineering
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream enzyme for supplying methylated starter-CoA esters in microbial production pathways, but less decisive target than CHS in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Purified Os4CL was used to synthesize feruloyl-CoA for in vitro HvCHS assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os4CL provided activated methylated hydroxycinnamate substrates for downstream methylated flavonoid synthesis. 2. The HvCHS+Os4CL pathway outperformed the PhCHS+Pc4CL pathway and uniquely produced hesperetin in the initial test. 3. Although engineered further, Os4CL was not the main determinant of final titer improvements.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: hydroxycinnamic acid activation for methylated flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: increases supply of activated methylated precursor to downstream methylated flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered variant intended to enlarge substrate-binding pocket for better activation of O-methylated hydroxycinnamic acids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ferulic acid; isoferulic acid; ATP; coenzyme A
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in engineered E. coli after IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of V340 increased homoeriodictyol titer about 1.5-fold compared with wild-type Os4CL combination, whereas other tested 4CL mutants were neutral or detrimental
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; heterologous pathway screening in E. coli; HPLC metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fed methylated hydroxycinnamic acid precursors; IPTG induction; cerulenin supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: benefit was modest and did not translate into dominant best-performing pathway compared with CHS engineering
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Shows upstream substrate-pocket engineering of 4CL can modestly improve methylated flavonoid production but should likely be combined with CHS engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Based on analogy to Nicotiana tabacum 4CL mutagenesis enabling larger methoxylated substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os4CL(delV340) modestly improved homoeriodictyol production in vivo. 2. The mutation was designed to create more space for O-methylated substrates. 3. Compared with CHS engineering, its impact on overall pathway output was limited.
